name: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer with Pseudomonas sp. prey (predation batch) (PMC12229818)
description: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer with Pseudomonas sp. prey (predation batch) — medium reported in PMC12229818, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC12229818 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (Bdellovibrio predation buffer)' (Full composition stated verbatim in this paper's Materials and Methods (PMC12229818). The buffer is the standard Ca/HEPES Bdellovibrio predation buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, pH 7.6; e.g. Lambert C. & Sockett R.E., and Lambert et al. 2006 Appl Environ Microbiol 72:6757-6765, PMID:17021229), here additionally supplemented with 3 mM MgCl2.). Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + s
provenance.verification_evidence: "Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6)". Used for Pseudomonas sp. prey + B. bacteriovorus predation batches; all cultures aerobic at 30 degC.
provenance.recipe_evidence: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6). Prey organism: Pseudomonas sp. (DSM no. 50906). Predator: Bdellovibrio bacteriovorus HD100; cultures incubated at 30 degrees C with shaking at 200 rpm (aerobic).
provenance.base_medium_reference: Full composition stated verbatim in this paper's Materials and Methods (PMC12229818). The buffer is the standard Ca/HEPES Bdellovibrio predation buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, pH 7.6; e.g. Lambert C. & Sockett R.E., and Lambert et al. 2006 Appl Environ Microbiol 72:6757-6765, PMID:17021229), here additionally supplemented with 3 mM MgCl2.

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
